groups.title: Essentials
groups.description: Core brief fields for a useful Facebook post.

groups.title: Message & Conversion
groups.description: Links, offers, proof, and time-sensitive details.

groups.title: Brand & Delivery
groups.description: Voice, length, emoji, hashtags, variants, and visual concept options.

groups.title: Trust & Restrictions
groups.description: Disclosures, sensitive topics, and additional constraints.

variables.label: What is the post about?
variables.placeholder: A product update, event, business news, useful tip, offer, story, or announcement
variables.hint: Name the main topic, product, service, event, company, or idea.

help.what: The main topic, product, service, event, company, offer, story, or idea for the post.
help.why: It establishes the central subject of the Facebook post.
help.example: A product update, event, business news, useful tip, offer, story, or announcement
help.avoid: Several unrelated subjects or vague topics like "content".

variables.label: Post type
variables.placeholder: Choose a format or let the model decide
variables.hint: Select the closest format or use Auto for goal-based selection.

help.why: Different Facebook post types require different pacing, openings, and content structures.
help.example: Choose a format or let the model decide
help.avoid: Choosing a promotional format when the supplied information is not promotional.

variables.label: Primary goal
variables.placeholder: Choose the main result you want
variables.hint: Select one main objective so the post stays focused.

help.why: The goal determines the post angle, emphasis, and appropriate call to action.
help.example: Choose the main result you want
help.avoid: Stacking several unrelated goals into one Facebook post.

variables.label: Target audience
variables.placeholder: Who should care about this post, and what matters to them?
variables.hint: Describe the intended readers, customers, members, or community.

help.why: Audience context materially changes language, examples, relevance, and persuasion.
help.example: Who should care about this post, and what matters to them?
help.avoid: Overbroad audiences such as "everyone" or sensitive targeting assumptions.

variables.label: Main message
variables.placeholder: What should readers understand, remember, or feel after reading?
variables.hint: State the central takeaway in your own words.

help.why: It prevents the generated post from drifting away from the intended point.
help.example: What should readers understand, remember, or feel after reading?
help.avoid: A generic slogan with no clear takeaway.

variables.label: Essential facts
variables.placeholder: Add accurate details, dates, features, prices, names, conditions, or context
variables.hint: Include only facts that may safely appear in the published post.

help.why: It supplies the factual foundation and prevents invented details.
help.example: Add accurate details, dates, features, prices, names, conditions, or context
help.avoid: Invented facts, prices, dates, names, testimonials, or product claims.

variables.label: Output language
variables.placeholder: Choose the publication language
variables.hint: The complete generated package will use this language.

help.why: The publication language cannot always be safely inferred from the topic.
help.example: Choose the publication language
help.avoid: Mixing languages unless that is intentionally requested.

variables.label: Publishing context
variables.placeholder: Where will the post appear?
variables.hint: The writing style will adapt to the selected Facebook environment.

help.why: Pages, Groups, Profiles, and Event Pages have different audience expectations and levels of formality.
help.example: Where will the post appear?
help.avoid: Assuming Pages, Groups, Profiles, and Event Pages behave the same way.

variables.label: Tone
variables.placeholder: Choose the overall writing tone
variables.hint: The tone will be adapted without making the post sound artificial.

help.why: Tone is a genuine brand and communication preference that cannot always be inferred.
help.example: Choose the overall writing tone
help.avoid: Exaggerated tone that makes the post sound artificial or manipulative.

variables.label: Desired reader action
variables.placeholder: What should readers do next?
variables.hint: Choose one action or let the model select a proportionate CTA.

help.why: It determines whether the post should end with a CTA, discussion prompt, or simple conclusion.
help.example: What should readers do next?
help.avoid: Multiple competing CTAs or engagement bait.

variables.label: Destination URL
variables.placeholder: https://example.com/page
variables.hint: Add the exact page readers should visit. Leave blank when no link is needed.

help.why: A supplied destination changes the CTA and allows accurate link-placement guidance.
help.example: https://example.com/page
help.avoid: Invented URLs, hidden affiliate links, or unverified destinations.

variables.label: What is on the linked page?
variables.placeholder: Product page, article, registration form, event details, download, or booking page
variables.hint: Explain what readers will find after clicking.

help.what: The specific destination readers will reach if they open the supplied link.
help.why: It enables an accurate transition to the link without guessing what the destination contains.
help.example: Product page, article, registration form, event details, download, or booking page
help.avoid: Claiming the destination contains something that was not supplied.

variables.label: Offer details
variables.placeholder: Price, discount, eligibility, availability, included items, and genuine deadline
variables.hint: Include only confirmed terms that may be published.

help.why: Promotional posts require precise terms and must not invent prices, scarcity, or deadlines.
help.example: Price, discount, eligibility, availability, included items, and genuine deadline
help.avoid: Fake scarcity, unsupported discounts, invented deadlines, or unclear payment terms.

variables.label: Approved proof points
variables.placeholder: Verified results, statistics, customer feedback, credentials, or product evidence
variables.hint: Add only claims, reviews, or evidence you are authorized to publish.

help.why: It allows credible support while clearly limiting the model to approved evidence.
help.example: Verified results, statistics, customer feedback, credentials, or product evidence
help.avoid: Unapproved reviews, statistics, customer names, screenshots, ratings, or results.

variables.label: Timing or date details
variables.placeholder: Launch date, event time, registration deadline, opening hours, or availability period
variables.hint: Add exact timing only when it matters to the post.

help.why: It prevents the model from inventing deadlines, dates, or availability.
help.example: Launch date, event time, registration deadline, opening hours, or availability period
help.avoid: Relative dates such as "tomorrow" without an absolute date and time zone.

variables.label: Brand voice
variables.placeholder: Casual and direct, expert but approachable, playful, calm, community-focused
variables.hint: Describe established voice traits or paste a brief style reference.

help.why: An established brand voice may differ from the general tone selection.
help.example: Casual and direct, expert but approachable, playful, calm, community-focused
help.avoid: A voice note that contradicts factual, privacy, or disclosure requirements.

variables.label: Post length
variables.placeholder: Choose a preferred depth
variables.hint: Auto selects a practical length based on the post’s goal and information.

help.why: Users may have a genuine preference for a brief update or a more developed post.
help.example: Choose a preferred depth
help.avoid: Padding a simple update just to hit a longer target.

variables.label: Emoji use
variables.placeholder: Choose how often emojis may appear
variables.hint: Emojis will be used only when they fit the tone and subject.

help.why: Emoji preferences materially affect the visual tone of social content.
help.example: Choose how often emojis may appear
help.avoid: Emoji chains or emojis in serious, regulated, or sensitive content.

variables.label: Hashtags
variables.placeholder: Choose whether hashtags should be included
variables.hint: Facebook hashtags will remain limited and relevant.

help.why: Some brands use Facebook hashtags while others deliberately avoid them.
help.example: Choose whether hashtags should be included
help.avoid: Hashtag walls, trending tags unrelated to the post, or competitor tags.

variables.label: Number of versions
variables.hint: Generate one to three meaningfully different post versions.

help.why: Some users need alternatives for approval or testing, while one version remains the simple default.
help.avoid: Requesting tiny synonym swaps instead of meaningfully different angles.

variables.label: Include visual concept
variables.placeholder: Not applicable
variables.hint: Adds one practical image or video concept that supports the post.

help.why: Facebook posts often benefit from a supporting visual, but not every workflow requires one.
help.example: On
help.avoid: Fabricated screenshots, fake reviews, misleading before-and-after visuals, or unauthorized people.

variables.label: Commercial relationship
variables.placeholder: Select any relevant affiliation
variables.hint: Used to add transparent disclosure when promotion or affiliation is relevant.

help.why: It helps prevent covert advertising and undisclosed commercial promotion.
help.example: Select any relevant affiliation
help.avoid: Hiding sponsorship, affiliate, employer, client, or own-product relationships.

variables.label: Sensitive subject area
variables.placeholder: Choose a regulated or high-stakes area
variables.hint: Applies cautious wording and avoids unsupported professional advice.

help.why: High-stakes subjects require stronger accuracy, safety, and claim-handling rules.
help.example: Choose a regulated or high-stakes area
help.avoid: Personalized professional advice or guaranteed outcomes in high-stakes areas.

variables.label: Additional requirements
variables.placeholder: Required wording, prohibited claims, names to include, privacy limits, or anything to avoid
variables.hint: Add only requirements not already covered by another field.

help.why: It provides one controlled place for unique restrictions without creating multiple miscellaneous fields.
help.example: Required wording, prohibited claims, names to include, privacy limits, or anything to avoid
help.avoid: Repeating existing fields or adding requirements that conflict with accuracy, safety, privacy, or platform integrity.
